title: cicuta virosa var. californica
intro: Cicuta virosa var. californica, also known as California water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to the western United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and emetic, although its toxicity can be hazardous if ingested in large quantities. In small amounts, the plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herbal preparations of Cicuta virosa var. californica include tinctures, infusions, and poultices, although its use requires extreme caution and should only be undertaken under the guidance of a trained medical professional.
